channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'House of the Dragon,' Season 3, Episode 4: "Now we begin?" It's Season 3!
item.description: Will the real Daeron please stand up?
item.content:encoded:
Will the real Daeron please stand up?

(Image credit: Theo Whiteman)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Two words, same number of letters, matching first and last letters
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz and WVXU listener Debra Waller of Burlington, Kentucky.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz and WVXU listener Debra Waller of Burlington, Kentucky.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: 'Good, clean community fun': Dance halls are making a comeback across Wyoming
item.description: Community square dance halls were long cherished social institutions in Western states where small populations are spread over vast distances. Dance halls in Wyoming are making a comeback.
item.content:encoded: Community square dance halls were long cherished social institutions in Western states where small populations are spread over vast distances. Dance halls in Wyoming are making a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Merzbach

item.title: An artist brought 'I.C.E. pops' to a Texas campus. The show was shut down in days
item.description: The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration's executive orders have meant that administrators are questioning what art can — and can't — be seen on campus.

(Image credit: Laila AnnMarie Stevens for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Azar Nafisi on the movie adaptation of her memoir 'Reading Lolita in Tehran'
item.description: In an interview with NPR, memoirist Azar Nafisi reflects on the enduring power of literature.
item.content:encoded:
Reading Lolita in Tehran.'/>

In an interview with NPR, memoirist Azar Nafisi reflects on the enduring power of literature.

(Image credit: Marie Gioanni/Greenwich Entertainment)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Shy on the dance floor? Virtual reality 'partners' aim to help you find your groove
item.description: VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.
item.content:encoded:
VR dance lesson apps like Dance Guru and Trip the Light offer a judgment-free way to learn partner dancing.

(Image credit: Chloe Veltman)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Terry Tempest Williams on what it means to be a woman with a big voice and big ideas
item.description: Terry Tempest Williams writes about what is easy to overlook – she calls it "the holy ordinary." She spoke with Wild Card's Rachel Martin about being present and her new book, The Glorians.
item.content:encoded: Terry Tempest Williams writes about what is easy to overlook – she calls it "the holy ordinary." She spoke with Wild Card's Rachel Martin about being present and her new book, The Glorians.

item.title: Patriotic art gets the spotlight as NEA funding shifts. Cue 'The Ronald Reagan Overture'
item.description: Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.
item.content:encoded:
Patriotic art and music is taking center stage this year under the Trump Administration, as funds shift away from DEI. For some orgs, like the Reagan Presidential Library, this is their wheelhouse.

(Image credit: Consolidated News Pictures/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Colin Farrell plays a P.I. with the strangest of secrets in 'Sugar' Season 2
item.description: The Apple TV series wraps noir inside science fiction. With subtlety and charm, Farrell plays an earnest alien just doing his best as a private eye in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded: The Apple TV series wraps noir inside science fiction. With subtlety and charm, Farrell plays an earnest alien just doing his best as a private eye in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: John Powers

item.title: New 'Little House' remake will inspire you to rewatch the '70s TV series
item.description: Netflix's new Little House series features the same characters and setting as the original, but its reliance on hand-held cameras, in extreme close-up, calls too much attention to itself.
item.content:encoded:
Netflix's new Little House series features the same characters and setting as the original, but its reliance on hand-held cameras, in extreme close-up, calls too much attention to itself.

(Image credit: Eric Zachanowich)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli
